title: तारा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीतारास्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ ह्रीं स्त्रीं हुं फट्।

तारां तारयितुं शक्तां संसारार्णवपारगाम्।
नीलसरस्वतीं वन्दे महाविद्यां सनातनीम्॥१॥

उग्रतारां महाभीमां नीलवर्णां दिगम्बराम्।
मुण्डमालाविभूषाढ्यां शववाहनसंस्थिताम्॥२॥

कर्त्रीखड्गकपालाब्जधारिणीं घोररूपिणीम्।
प्रलयानलसंकाशां जटाजूटविराजिताम्॥३॥

त्रिनेत्रां रक्तवदनां दंष्ट्राकरालभीषणाम्।
श्मशानवासिनीं देवीं तारां प्रणमयाम्यहम्॥४॥

सर्वविद्याप्रदां देवीं सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनीम्।
वाग्बलैश्वर्यदात्रीं च तारां वन्दे सुरार्चिताम्॥५॥

अविद्यातिमिरं हन्तुं विद्यारूपां प्रकाशिनीम्।
ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवैर्वन्द्यां तारां नौमि जगद्धिताम्॥६॥

भवसागरमग्नानां जीवानामार्तिनाशिनीम्।
तारयन्तीं परं श्रेयस्तारां वन्दे कृपानिधिम्॥७॥

एकजटां महाविद्यां नीलसरस्वतीमहम्।
द्वितीयां दशविद्यासु तारां नमामि भक्तितः॥८॥

हरेणुकासमुद्भूतां पीठस्थां सागरोपमाम्।
वज्रवैरोचनीं देवीं तारां प्रणमयाम्यहम्॥९॥

सर्वमन्त्रमयीं शक्तिं सर्वयन्त्रनिवासिनीम्।
सर्वतन्त्रमयीं देवीं तारां वन्दे सदाशिवाम्॥१०॥

तारास्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वविद्यामवाप्नोति तारालोकं च गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्रीतारास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Taaraa Stotram ||

Om Hreem Streem Hum Phat.

Taaraam Taarayitum Shaktaam Samsaaraarnava-Paaragaam.
Neelasarasvateem Vande Mahaavidyaam Sanaataneem.||1||

Ugrataaraam Mahaabheemaam Neelavarnaam Digambaraam.
Mundamaalaa-Vibhooshaadhyaam Shavavaahanasamsthitaam.||2||

Kartree-Khadga-Kapaalaabja-Dhaarineem Ghoraroopineem.
Pralayaanala-Samkaashaam Jataajootaviraajitaam.||3||

Trinetraam Raktavadanaam Damshtraakaraala-Bheeshanaam.
Shmashaanavaaseeneem Deveem Taaraam Pranamayaamyaham.||4||

Sarvavidyaapradaam Deveem Sarvasiddhipradaayineem.
Vaagbalaishvaryadaatrim Cha Taaraam Vande Suraarchitaam.||5||

Avidyaatimaram Hantum Vidyaaroopaam Prakaashineem.
Brahma-Vishnu-Shivairvandhyaam Taaraam Naumi Jagaddhitaam.||6||

Bhavasaagaramagnaanam Jeevaanaam-Aartinashineem.
Taarayanteem Param Shreyas-Taaraam Vande Kripaanidhim.||7||

EkaJataam Mahaavidyaam Neelasarasvateem-Aham.
Dviteeyaam Dashavidyaasu Taaraam Namaami Bhaktitah.||8||

Harenukaasamudbhootaam Peethasthaam Saagaropamaam.
Vajravairoochaneem Deveem Taaraam Pranamayaamyaham.||9||

Sarvamantramayeem Shaktim Sarvayantra-Nivaasineem.
Sarvatantramayeem Deveem Taaraam Vande Sadaashivaam.||10||

Taaraa-Stotramidam Punyam Yah Pathethbhaktisamyutah.
Sarvavidyaamavaapnoti Taaralokam Cha Gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Taaraa Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
मूल मन्त्र: ॐ ह्रीं स्त्रीं हुं फट्।

१. संसार-सागर से पार करने में शक्त तारा को, नीलसरस्वती को, सनातनी महाविद्या को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

२. उग्रतारा, महाभीम, नीलवर्णा, दिगम्बरा, मुण्डमाला से विभूषित, शव-वाहन पर स्थित।

३. कर्त्री (कैंची), खड्ग, कपाल, कमल धारण करने वाली, घोररूपिणी, प्रलयाग्नि के समान, जटाजूट से विराजित।

४. त्रिनेत्रा, रक्तवदना, दंष्ट्रा-करालभीषणा, श्मशानवासिनी देवी तारा को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ।

५. सर्वविद्याप्रदा, सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी, वाक्-बल-ऐश्वर्य दात्री, सुरार्चित तारा को वन्दन करता हूँ।

६. अविद्या-अन्धकार का नाश करने के लिए विद्यारूपा प्रकाशिनी, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शिव द्वारा वन्द्य, जगत्-हितकारिणी तारा को प्रणाम।

७. भवसागर में डूबे जीवों की पीड़ा नाश करने वाली, परम श्रेय की ओर तारने वाली कृपानिधि तारा को वन्दन।

८. एकजटा, महाविद्या, नीलसरस्वती — दश विद्याओं में द्वितीय — तारा को मैं भक्ति से नमस्कार करता हूँ।

९. हरेणुका से उद्भूत, पीठस्था, सागर-समान, वज्र-वैरोचनी देवी तारा को प्रणाम करता हूँ।

१०. सर्वमन्त्रमयी शक्ति, सर्वयन्त्रनिवासिनी, सर्वतन्त्रमयी देवी, सदाशिवा तारा को वन्दन।

जो भक्तियुक्त होकर इस पुण्य तारा-स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वविद्या प्राप्त करता है और तारालोक को जाता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीtarastotramमन्त्रस्य अक्षोभ्य ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री तारा (दश महाविद्या) देवता। ॐ ह्रीं स्त्रीं हुं फट् बीजम्। तारा शक्तिः। भवसागर-तारण एवं सर्वविद्या प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीताराप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Tara is the 2nd Mahavidya — She who saves and ferries across the ocean of existence
Also known as Neel Saraswati — grants all knowledge and wisdom
Traditionally recited for removal of the darkness of ignorance (avidya) with the light of vidya
Worshipped by Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva themselves
Invokes vak siddhi, physical strength, and lordship
Encompasses all Mantras, Yantras, and Tantras within Her being
Powerful for crossing life crises and spiritual emergencies
description: Dedicated to Goddess Kali, the Tara Stotram (तारा स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Tantrasara and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to tara is the 2nd Mahavidya — She who saves and ferries across the ocean of existence and grant Vak Siddhi, physical strength, and lordship. It is also said to encompass all Mantras, Yantras, and Tantras within Her being. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Navratri and Kali Puja. With 10 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Tara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Tara Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Ten verses to the 2nd Mahavidya Neel Saraswati for knowledge, protection, and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Tara in the Dasha Mahavidya tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Tara is the second of the ten Mahavidyas — the ten great wisdom goddesses of the tantric tradition — following Kali. She represents the goddess as the saviour who carries devotees across the ocean of samsara (the Sanskrit root tr meaning to cross) and as the supreme power of speech, sound and mantra (Vak-shakti). Tara appears in three principal forms: Ekajata (single-braided, fierce), Ugratara (the wrathful) and Nila Saraswati (the dark Saraswati, granter of wisdom). She is depicted as dark blue or deep blue-green, standing on a pratyalidha posture on a corpse, holding a scimitar, lotus, scissors and skull cup, with a garland of severed heads. Her chief seat is Tarapith in Birbhum, West Bengal. (Sources: Wikipedia Tara Mahavidya; Hindupedia Dasha Mahavidya; Tantrasara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Tara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tara Stotram is preserved in classical tantric literature, particularly the Tantrasara compiled by Krishnananda Agamavagisha in 16th-century Bengal — the great compendium of Shakta-tantric ritual that systematised the worship of Kali, Tara and the other Mahavidyas. Other significant sources include the Mahanirvana Tantra, the Brihat Tantrasara, the Rudra Yamala, and the Mundamala Tantra, each preserving stotras to Tara that are recited in different lineages. The version most widely circulated in printed pothi today is from the Tantrasara recension. The Tara stotras attributed to sage Vasishtha — who is said in tradition to have rediscovered the Chinachara form of Tara worship — are also widely studied. (Sources: Hindupedia Tantrasara; Wikipedia Tara; Sanskritdocuments.org.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Tara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Saturday are the principal weekdays for Tara worship, drawing on her connection to Mars (warrior energy) and Saturn (transformation through dissolution). Amavasya is the supreme tithi, especially Kaushiki Amavasya in Bhadrapada and Phalguna Amavasya. Annual peak occasions include Tara Jayanti on Chaitra Shukla Navami in some lineages, Caitra Navaratri and the Sharad Navaratri (especially the second night when Tara is worshipped). Tantric practitioners often recite the stotram at midnight (nishita kaal) or the Brahma muhurta hour before dawn, especially at shakti-pithas like Tarapith. Devotees seeking mantra-siddhi, mastery of speech, or success in scholarship and writing often recite it daily. (Sources: Drikpanchang; Hindupedia Tarapith; Tantrasara.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Tara Stotram phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Tara Stotram promises that the devotee gains mastery of speech (vak-siddhi), success in scholarship and debate, removal of stammer and hesitation, protection from enemies and planetary afflictions, victory in legal proceedings, and the destruction of all sins of the present life and previous births. It is particularly noted as the supreme stotra for moksha during Kali Yuga — the Tantrasara states that as Tara is the saviour goddess (literally the one who carries across), her stotra recited with faith liberates the devotee from samsara. Specific benefits also include success in mantra-japa of any deity, mantra-siddhi, fearlessness, and the removal of pretadi badhas (afflictions by ghosts and elementals). (Sources: Tantrasara; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chinachara connection to Tara worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical tradition records that the worship of Tara was first received by sage Vasishtha through Chinachara — a method of Tara sadhana said to have come from the Mahachina region (variously identified with Tibet or northern frontier traditions). The story holds that Vasishtha, unable to attain Tara-siddhi through Vedic methods alone, travelled to Mahachina where Lord Buddha or Vishnu in a Buddha-rupa instructed him in the appropriate kaula-tantric methods. From this exchange the Chinachara tradition entered Indian Shakta practice. Tara accordingly retains striking iconographic and methodological parallels with Vajrayana Tara worship in Tibetan Buddhism, though the underlying theology of Tara as Adi-Shakti remains distinctively Shakta. (Sources: Wikipedia Tara Mahavidya; Hindupedia; Brihat Tantrasara.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Tara Stotram safe for householders to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram itself — as a praise hymn distinct from formal tantric kriya — is open to householders, particularly the bhakti-marga recitation with vegetarian offerings of red hibiscus, blue lotus and rice. Across Bengal, Assam and Mithila, householders recite the Tara Stotram daily or weekly for family protection, wisdom and prosperity. The vama-marga or fierce tantric recitations involving cremation-ground rituals (especially at Tarapith), panchamakara offerings, or specific bija-mantra invocations of Strim or Hum-Phat are restricted to initiated practitioners under qualified guru guidance. For ordinary devotion, a clean room facing east, lighting a lamp before a Tara murti or yantra, and reciting with bhakti is the universally accepted practice. (Sources: Hindupedia Tara; Tantrasara; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Tara and Saraswati?
seoFaqs.answer: While both are goddesses associated with speech, learning and wisdom, they belong to distinct tantric streams. Saraswati of the Vedic and Brahmanical tradition is the gentle, white-clad consort of Brahma, holder of the veena, embodiment of pure sattvic learning. Tara as Nila Saraswati (the dark Saraswati) is her tantric counterpart in the Mahavidya tradition — fierce, dark-blue, granting both worldly speech-mastery and the ultimate transcendence of language into pure sound (Para-vak). Tara's wisdom is the wisdom of dissolution and liberation through tantric methods, while Saraswati's wisdom is the wisdom of accumulation and refinement through Vedic study. The two are complementary aspects of Vak-shakti rather than competing goddesses. (Sources: Hindupedia Tara Saraswati; Wikipedia Mahavidya; Tantrasara.)
